content.full_text:
/

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 17, 1998

Gerald F. Hicks
74 Amberwood Lane
Walnut Creek, California 94598

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-98-181

Dear Mr. Hicks:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You write on behalf of your wife, who is running for
reelection to the Walnut Creek City Council, and who anticipates that persons appointed to
public office by the city council may wish to contribute to her election campaign.

QUESTIONS AND CONCLUSIONS

1. Since Proposition 208 has been enjoined by the court, may city councilmembers
accept campaign contributions from appointed city commissioners?

Section 85705, added to the Act by Proposition 208, would have prohibited contributions
to councilmembers from appointed city commissioners. On January 6, 1998, the Federal District
Court for the Eastern District of California enjoined enforcement of Proposition 208, pending
final adjudication of the measure's constitutionality. California ProLife Council Political Action
Committee v. Scully, 989 F.Supp. 1282 (E.D. Calif. 1998). This preliminary injunction remains
in effect while under appeal to the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals.

The district court's injunction has the effect of delaying or suspending implementation of
Proposition 208. Heart of Atlanta Motel, Inc. v. United States, 85 S.Ct. 1, 2 (1964). Section
85705 is therefore without effect and cannot prohibit the contributions you describe, so long as
the injunction remains in force. Any such contributions may, of course, be subject to other
provisions of state and local law.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-98-181
Page 2

2. If the provisions of Proposition 208 are determined to be constitutional and
enforceable prior to the end of the present election cycle, could a candidate return contributions
received from city commissioners and still be in compliance with Proposition 208?

If the injunction is vacated before the election, a new effective date will be established
for Proposition 208. Contributions permissible but for Proposition 208, which will be made and
received before the new effective date, will not give rise to a violation, and need not be returned.

If you have any other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
